methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Theion is a Series A–stage climate and energy company founded by Ulrich Ehmes that has raised €25M+ to date. The peer universe consists of four same-sector, same-stage private companies: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), supplemented by a broader comparable set of eight private firms and one public peer (LNZA).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the four identified Stage A climate and energy comparables is $175M, with a range spanning $110M (Steady Energy floor) to $1.8B (Watershed ceiling). This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business models and de-risking within the sector. The peer-median result of $175M anchors the analysis at the midpoint of observed market pricing for functionally similar–stage companies.

The triangulated valuation range supports a median of $175M, with a low bound of $149M and a high bound of $201M. This 15% band around the median reflects the limited methodological diversity available and inherent uncertainty in peer selection. Key downside risks include sector-specific execution risk and whether Theion's technology profile justifies positioning relative to the peer set. The wide interquartile spread in comparable valuations underscores that stage and sector alone do not fully constrain valuation; differentiated traction, IP, or market timing materially drive outcomes within this range.
